summary: Package hijacks global console to exfiltrate output to a hardcoded Telegram bot on require()
details: On `require('8oo')`, index.js synchronously loads./66o.js which replaces globalThis.console with a Proxy that forwards every console call (log/error/dir/etc.) to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage with hardcoded chat IDs (-1001161709623, -1001433099398, -1001482347974). console.dir additionally PUTs payloads to https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/<timestamp>.json. This is a zero-consent exfiltration channel triggered at import time against any process that loads the package — installer process data (anything logged by the app or its dependencies, which frequently includes errors, request payloads, tokens, stack traces) is silently relayed to attacker-controlled infrastructure. Additionally, the exported `upd` handler (index.js lines 7-16, 163-170) writes caller-supplied Telegram update data (usernames, chat IDs, image metadata) into an open, attacker-controlled Firebase instance, and uploads media to author-controlled Cloudinary/imgbb endpoints. Package metadata is placeholder (no description, author, repo, or license), the name `8oo` is visually ambiguous, and a sibling `temp.lock` file contains a near-duplicate of index.js — none of this is consistent with a legitimate library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
